---
title: "`r params$report_title`"
author: "`r params$report_author`"
date: "`r Sys.Date()`"
output:
  html_document:
    theme: flatly
    toc: false
    code_folding: hide
params:
  # Engine flag: TRUE only when this template is stitched into the
  # combined report (set by the parent). Leave false for normal renders.
  combined_mode: false
  # Master Data Path
  data_path: !r here::here("1_data", "master_micro_survey.csv")

  # Variables
  group_var: "income_tier"
  value_var: "endline_score"

  # Readability guard: keep only the N most frequent groups, lump the rest
  # into "Other" (prevents 20+ facilities from overcrowding the Y-axis)
  max_groups: 8

  # Policy-Facing Labels
  metric_name: "Endline Test Score"

  # --- Report Text (all editable; leave "" to use the smart default) ---
  report_title: "Distribution Landscape: Ridgeline Plot"
  report_author: "Ibraheem Saqib Ellahi"
  chart_title: ""
  chart_subtitle: ""
  x_label: ""
  y_label: ""
  source_note: "Generated via CERP Analytics Template"
---

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(
  echo = !isTRUE(params$combined_mode),
  warning = FALSE,
  message = FALSE,
  dev.args = list(bg = "#FAF9F6"),
  fig.path = paste0(here::here("4_output", "figures"), "/"),
  fig.width = 9,
  fig.height = 6,
  fig.retina = 3
)
```

```{r cerp-style, echo=FALSE, results='asis', eval=!isTRUE(params$combined_mode)}
cat("<style>", readr::read_file(here::here("2_R", "cerp_style.css")), "</style>")
```

```{r load-and-prep}
library(tidyverse)
library(ggridges)
source(here::here("2_R", "2.5_helpers.R"), local = TRUE)

# Load data (headers and values whitespace-scrubbed) and validate YAML params.
# All data scrubbing + plotting now lives in viz_ridgeline() (2_R/2.6_viz_functions.R).
raw_data <- cerp_load(params$data_path)
cerp_validate(raw_data, params[grepl("_var$", names(params))])

# Build the plot object now so the chunk below can size fig.height from the
# number of ridges it actually contains (nlevels of p$data$Group).
source(here::here("2_R", "2.0_setup_theme.R"), local = TRUE)
source(here::here("2_R", "2.6_viz_functions.R"), local = TRUE)

p <- viz_ridgeline(
  data           = raw_data,
  group_var      = params$group_var,
  value_var      = params$value_var,
  max_groups     = params$max_groups,
  metric_name    = params$metric_name,
  chart_title    = params$chart_title,
  chart_subtitle = params$chart_subtitle,
  x_label        = params$x_label,
  y_label        = params$y_label,
  source_note    = params$source_note
)
```


```{r plot-ridgeline, fig.height=max(4.5, 1 + 0.6 * nlevels(p$data$Group))}
p
```
